placeholders.custom: Enter a hex color value, for example #1677ff

placeholders.name: Please enter a job name
placeholders.timezone: Please enter or select a timezone

summary.daysWithValue: Keep logs from the last {value} day(s)

retention.followSystemHelp:
Follow system: the job cleanup follows the system-level log retention policy.
Current system policy: {currentPolicy}.
If the job defines an override, the job-level policy takes precedence.

help.cron:
Supports 5-part or 6-part Cron.
5-part expressions are parsed as minute hour day month week, and the runtime fills the seconds placeholder automatically.
6-part expressions can set seconds explicitly.
help.timeout:
Maximum runtime allowed for a single job instance.
When the limit is exceeded, the system attempts to interrupt the execution and marks the execution log as timed out.
Leave some headroom above the normal runtime to avoid false positives.
help.maxExecutions:
Limits the accumulated execution count that participates in execution counting.
Once the limit is reached, the job is disabled automatically and the stop reason is recorded.
Set to 0 to disable the limit; manual Run Now actions do not increase the count.
help.scope:
Primary Node Only: only the current primary node runs the job.
All Nodes: every online node runs the job once.
help.concurrency:
Singleton: if one instance is already running on the current node, new triggers are skipped.
Parallel: multiple instances can run on the current node, limited by Max Concurrency.

messages.pluginPausedTooltip: This job depends on a handler provided by a plugin. When the plugin is disabled, uninstalled, or the handler is not registered, the system pauses the job automatically until the plugin becomes available again.

help.path: Use a relative route path for internal pages, or a full URL for external links.
help.permission: Use a unique permission key so menus and buttons can participate in unified authorization.
help.queryParam: Optional query parameters appended to the route, for example id=1&type=detail.
help.sort: Smaller values appear earlier in menus of the same level.
help.status: Disabled menus are hidden from regular navigation and cannot be opened normally.
help.visible: Hidden menus keep route access but do not appear in the sidebar.

columnHelp.type: Source plugins are delivered with host source code and build artifacts for built-in or trusted extensions; dynamic plugins are uploaded as wasm artifacts for runtime install, upgrade, and isolated governance.
columnHelp.runtimeState: Runtime state shows whether discovered plugin files, the database-effective version, upgrade flow, and abnormal diagnostics are consistent. The Status column only shows whether the plugin is currently enabled for business entry.
columnHelp.mockData: Yes means the plugin ships bundled mock data that can be loaded during install; No means no bundled mock data is available.
columnHelp.supportsMultiTenant: Yes means the plugin declares multi-tenant governance support and can use tenant-scoped install mode and tenant-side enablement; No means it is managed only as a platform-global capability.
columnHelp.tenantProvisioning: Controls whether a tenant-scoped plugin is enabled automatically for newly created tenants. Only multi-tenant plugins installed in tenant-scoped mode can use this policy.

actions.installMockDataTooltip: When checked, the host loads the plugin's manifest/sql/mock-data SQL files in a single database transaction after the install SQL succeeds. Any failure rolls back only the mock data; the plugin install itself remains in effect. Mock data is intended for demo and feature validation, not production use.

auth.enableBanner: Review the latest host-service snapshot before enabling this plugin.
auth.installBanner: Review the plugin metadata below before installation.
auth.installBannerWithAuth: This plugin requests host services. Review the requested scopes before installation.

runtimeStateHint.normal: File metadata matches the database-effective state.
runtimeStateHint.pendingUpgrade: Effective version {effectiveVersion} is lower than discovered version {discoveredVersion}; an administrator must confirm runtime upgrade.
runtimeStateHint.upgradeFailed: The latest runtime upgrade failed. Fix the cause before retrying.

abnormalReason.discovered_version_lower_than_effective: File version is lower than the database-effective version

uninstall.title: Uninstall Plugin
uninstall.defaultWarning: Uninstalling this plugin removes the runtime registration information. Review the impact before continuing.
uninstall.dynamicWarning: Uninstalling a dynamic plugin removes the uploaded artifact and can optionally clear plugin-owned data and storage files.
uninstall.purgeStorageWarning: When checked, the host runs uninstall SQL and physically deletes plugin-owned table data, authorized storage files, and governance resource references. This cannot be restored after confirmation.
uninstall.sourceWarning: Uninstalling a source plugin removes its synchronized governance records and can optionally clear plugin-owned data and storage files.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Plugin
upgrade.banner: Upgrade can run plugin callbacks, upgrade SQL, governance synchronization, and effective release switching. Review version differences and risk hints before confirming.
upgrade.retryBanner: The previous upgrade failed. Fix the cause and make sure callbacks and SQL are safely retryable before confirming.
upgrade.resolveBeforeUpgrade: Resolve dependency blockers before upgrading this plugin.
